llm_reasoning: Suitability is fair. The strongest positive factors are the optimal soil pH (7.05) and zero frost risk, which removes a major threat to wheat. However, the strongest limiting factor is the very low annual rainfall (362.4 mm), insufficient for rainfed wheat, leading to significant water stress. Low soil organic carbon (10.96 g/kg) further limits water retention and fertility. Operational risks include drought-induced yield loss and low soil productivity. Mitigation requires supplemental irrigation, drought-tolerant varieties, conservation tillage, and organic matter enhancement. Moderate humidity also poses a fungal disease risk, necessitating resistant varieties and monitoring.
